title: Lastrea elwesii
intro: Lastrea elwesii, also known as Elwes' lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea elwesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elwes' lady fern is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to soothe respiratory issues and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lastrea elwesii is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. The genus Lastrea is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions.
